qEnglish: Cattle _______ driven all over the fields.
explanationEnglish: 'Cattle' is a collective noun that is always treated as plural and therefore takes a plural verb like 'are'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Her father accompanied by all her uncles _______ late.
explanationEnglish: When a singular subject ('Her father') is followed by terms like 'accompanied by', the parenthetical addition doesn't change the verb's singular agreement.
subject: English

qEnglish: The newspapers _______ it is going to rain all of next week.
explanationEnglish: 'The newspapers' is a plural subject noun phrase and takes the matching bare plural verb form 'say'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Both Mansi and Milli _____ the answer.
explanationEnglish: Subjects bound together using 'Both... and...' are strictly plural pluralities and require a root base verb match ('know').
subject: English

qEnglish: Either the boy or the girl _______ at fault.
explanationEnglish: When subjects are connected by 'either... or', the verb agrees with the closer subject. 'The girl' is singular, so it takes 'was'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Many researchers _______ conducted this experiment to prove their point.
explanationEnglish: The plural subject 'Many researchers' requires the plural auxiliary verb 'have' to form the active voice present perfect tense.
subject: English

qEnglish: After months of recession the economy _______ picking up.
explanationEnglish: The singular subject 'the economy' paired with the present continuous participle 'picking up' requires the auxiliary 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Fill up the blank - Plutarch's Lives _________ an interesting book.
explanationEnglish: Even though 'Plutarch's Lives' ends in an 's', it is the singular title of a single book, thus requiring the singular present verb 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Smoke, foul water, dirty air and litter _________ our environment just as much as noise.
explanationEnglish: The compound subject consists of multiple plural elements ('Smoke, foul water, dirty air and litter'), meaning it takes a matching plural verb 'Pollute'.
subject: English

qEnglish: The council that met in the Town Hall _______ divided.
explanationEnglish: When a collective noun ('The council') behaves as separate individuals experiencing disagreement or division, it takes a plural verb ('were').
subject: English

qEnglish: No person of that name _______ here.
explanationEnglish: The indefinite singular phrase phrase 'No person' requires a matching third-person singular present verb 'lives'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 1. My dues of seven thousand rupees..... to him.
explanationEnglish: According to the provided official answer key, 'has been paid' is the marked correct choice.
subject: English

qEnglish: 2. Everyone of the boys ......
explanationEnglish: Indefinite pronouns like 'everyone' function as singular subjects and require a singular verb form ('loves').
subject: English

qEnglish: 3. Choose the grammatically correct option
optionsEnglish: Any dictionaries will give you the meaning of these words.
Any dictionary will gives you the meaning of these words.
Any dictionary will give you the meaning of these words.
Any dictionary is give you the meaning of these words.
explanationEnglish: 'Any dictionary' is singular and requires the base form of the verb 'give' after the modal auxiliary 'will'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 4. Choose the grammatically correct option
optionsEnglish: Many a flowers is born to blush unseens.
Many a flowers are born to blush unseen.
Many a flower is born to blush unseen.
Many a flower are born to blush unseen.
explanationEnglish: The distributive phrase 'many a' is followed by a singular countable noun ('flower') and a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 5. Pick the correct sentence from the options given:
explanationEnglish: 'Nobody' takes a singular verb form ('lives') and 'forever' is spelled correctly as a single word.
subject: English

qEnglish: 6. Choose the correct answer form the following options determining whether subject agrees in number with the verb:
optionsEnglish: Just around the corner is a fantastic bakery and a small supermarket.
After all the gardening we did, the sun shining on the flowerbeds make a beautiful sight.
Jack, along with some of his closest friends, is sharing a car to attend a wedding.
There is according to my doctor, many courses of treatment available to me.
explanationEnglish: When a singular subject ('Jack') is joined with other nouns using 'along with', the verb remains singular ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish:
7. Fill in the blank with the correct option:
Either the boy or the girl ...... at fault.
explanationEnglish: When two singular subjects are connected by 'either... or', they take a singular verb ('was').
subject: English

qEnglish: 8. Ram as well as his brothers ...... coming today.
explanationEnglish: When subjects are joined by 'as well as', the verb agrees with the first subject ('Ram'), which is singular.
subject: English

qEnglish: 9. John as well as Tom ...... waiting for you.
explanationEnglish: The verb agrees with the first subject ('John'), which is singular, hence 'is' is used.
subject: English

qEnglish: 10. Each of the girl Scouts ...... a community project.
explanationEnglish: 'Each' takes a singular verb, which is marked as 'does' according to the answer key.
subject: English

qEnglish: 11. My friend, philosopher and guide ...... going abroad.
explanationEnglish: When multiple nouns refer to the same person, the subject is treated as singular and takes a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 12. A variety of beverages ...... flooded the market.
explanationEnglish: The expression 'a variety of' followed by a plural noun typically takes a plural verb ('have').
subject: English

qEnglish: 13. A hundred and twenty kilometers an hour ...... much too fast.
explanationEnglish: Expressions of distance or speed are treated as a single collective unit of measurement and take a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 14. Choose the correct sentence.
optionsEnglish: One of these candidates are sure to be selected.
One of these candidates is sure to be selected.
One of these candidates were sure to be selected.
One of these candidates have sure to be selected.
explanationEnglish: The subject is 'One', which is singular and requires the singular verb 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 15. Choose the correct sentence.
optionsEnglish: The class had divided in their opinions.
The class have divided in their opinions.
The class were divided in their opinions.
The class being divided in their opinions.
explanationEnglish: When the members of a collective noun function individually or are divided, a plural verb ('were') and plural pronouns are used.
subject: English

qEnglish: 16. One of my friends ...... going abroad.
explanationEnglish: The principal subject is 'One', which is singular and correctly takes the singular verb 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 17. Every boy and girl ...... (be) ready to appear for the examination.
explanationEnglish: Singular subjects connected by 'and' that are preceded by 'every' take a singular verb ('was').
subject: English

qEnglish: 18. Politics....... an interesting subject.
explanationEnglish: Nouns representing fields of study that end in '-ics' are singular in meaning and require a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 19. Eighty pounds a week ...... not a good wage.
explanationEnglish: An amount of money or singular wage sum is considered a unified expression and takes a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 20. The workers ...... worried about their jobs.
explanationEnglish: As per the official answer key, option (b) 'was' is marked as correct for this specific question paper sequence.
subject: English

qEnglish: 21. The jeans you sent me ...... fit me.
explanationEnglish: 'Jeans' is a plural-only noun and requires the plural negative auxiliary verb 'don't'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 22. Twelve hundred rupees ........ our sales price.
explanationEnglish: A collective sum of money acting as a single price point takes a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 23. Either the mayor or his deputy ...... bound to come.
explanationEnglish: When singular options are linked by 'either... or', they take a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 24. One of the boys ...... gone home.
explanationEnglish: The subject is 'One', which is singular and requires the singular auxiliary verb 'has'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 25. Renu, with her mother and sister..... spending a month in London.
explanationEnglish: Phrases introduced by 'with' do not pluralize the main subject ('Renu'), which takes a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 26. What I say and what I think ...... my own affairs.
explanationEnglish: Two separate noun clauses joined by 'and' represent distinct ideas and take a plural verb ('are').
subject: English

qEnglish: 27. The news....... true.
explanationEnglish: The noun 'news' is uncountable and always pairs with a singular verb form ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 28. Their means ...... simple.
explanationEnglish: When 'means' refers to income or wealth resources, it is treated as plural and takes a plural verb ('are').
subject: English

qEnglish: 29. A bunch of keys ...... lying on the table.
explanationEnglish: The subject is the singular collective noun 'bunch', requiring the singular verb 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 30. All citrus fruits ...... a rich source of vitamin C.
explanationEnglish: 'Fruits' is a plural subject and requires the plural verb 'are'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 31. ...... is lying on the table.
explanationEnglish: 'A new pair of glasses' correctly uses the singular structure 'pair' with the plural object 'glasses'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 32. Neither of the students ...... interested in extra classes.
explanationEnglish: The distributive pronoun 'neither' means 'none of the two' and takes a singular verb ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 33. One of my books ...... missing.
explanationEnglish: The primary subject is 'One', which dictates the use of the singular verb 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 34. The house with all its contents ...... insured.
explanationEnglish: The phrase introduced by 'with' does not affect the number of the singular main subject 'house', which takes 'was'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 35. Either he or I ...... mistaken.
explanationEnglish: When subjects are joined by 'either... or', the verb agrees with the closer subject pronoun ('I'), requiring 'am'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 36. Each of the students ...... responsible for maintaining the school garden.
explanationEnglish: The distributive pronoun 'Each' acts as a singular subject and takes a singular verb form ('is').
subject: English

qEnglish: 37. Neither she nor I ...... getting the Trophy this year.
explanationEnglish: With 'neither... nor', the verb matches the subject closest to it ('I'), which takes 'am'.
subject: English

qEnglish:
38. Select the most appropriate option to identify the incorrect segment in the following sentence:
The judge concluded that (a)/ either the maid or the driver were (b)/ responsible for the murder. (c)
explanationEnglish: Segment (b) contains the error because two singular subjects joined by 'either... or' must take the singular verb 'was' instead of 'were'.
subject: English

qEnglish:
39. Select the most appropriate option to identify the incorrect segment in the following sentence:
The dazzle and glamour (a)/ of money and success (b)/ have gone to her head.(c)
explanationEnglish: Segment (c) contains the error because 'The dazzle and glamour' forms a singular unified idea, requiring the singular verb 'has' instead of 'have'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 40. Choose the correct plural word from the list below.
explanationEnglish: The plural of compound nouns like 'mother-in-law' is formed by making the principal word plural, giving 'mothers-in-law'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 41. Choose the correct sentence in which the verb is in agreement with the subject -
optionsEnglish: The President and the Secretary was arrested.
Patience as well as perseverance are necessary for success.
Neither he nor his friends has broken the window pane.
The wages of sin is death.
explanationEnglish: The expression 'The wages of sin is death' is a traditional idiomatic sentence where 'wages' carries a singular meaning.
subject: English

qEnglish: 42. Choose the sentence with the correct subject-verb agreement -
optionsEnglish: The Committee have made its decision.
The Committee has made its decision.
The Committee has made their decision.
The Committee have made their decision.
explanationEnglish: When a collective noun like 'Committee' acts as a single cohesive unit, it takes a singular verb ('has') and a singular pronoun ('its').
subject: English

qEnglish: 43. Which sentence has the correct subject-verb agreement?
optionsEnglish: Neither of the boys has completed their homework.
Neither of the boys has completed his homework.
Neither of the boys have completed their homework.
Neither of the boy has completed their homework.
explanationEnglish: 'Neither of' takes a singular verb ('has') and is referred to by a singular possessive pronoun ('his').
subject: English

qEnglish:
44. Fill in the blank with the Verb that agrees with the subject in the sentence below-
The hammer and sickle....... from a tall flag post.
explanationEnglish: 'The hammer and sickle' acts as a single combined emblem emblem on a flag, hence taking a singular verb ('is flying').
subject: English

qEnglish: 45. Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct:
optionsEnglish: Neither the students nor the teachers has completed his work.
Neither the students nor the teacher have completed their work.
Neither the students nor the teacher has completed the work.
Neither the students nor the teacher have completed its work.
explanationEnglish: In a 'neither... nor' configuration, the verb agrees with the closer subject, which is the singular noun 'teacher', taking 'has'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 46. Identify the sentence with correct subject verb concord-
optionsEnglish: He go to temple every morning.
The ironsmith beats the iron daily.
explanationEnglish: The singular third-person subject 'The ironsmith' correctly takes the singular verb form 'beats'.
subject: English

qEnglish:
47. Select the part of the sentence that contains grammatical error -
New furniture were ordered for the primary classes.
explanationEnglish: 'Furniture' is an uncountable noun and must take a singular verb, so 'were ordered' is incorrect.
subject: English

qEnglish:
48. Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate pair of words:
Bread and butter ....... a soldier's food while honour and glory ....... the reward he awaits.
explanationEnglish: 'Bread and butter' represents a unified food item, and 'honour and glory' forms a singular compound abstract idea; both take 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish:
49. Fill in the blank with the correct option -
Either you or she ...... presenting a paper.
explanationEnglish: The verb agrees with the closer subject pronoun 'she', which requires the singular verb 'is'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 50. Which is that incorrect sentence.
optionsEnglish: It would be his last work.
He worked till hands began to tremble.
explanationEnglish: Option (C) is grammatically incorrect because a past participle like 'grown' requires a supporting auxiliary verb to function as a main verb.
subject: English

qEnglish: 51. Which of the following sentence is not correct?
optionsEnglish: We had swum across the river before the sunset.
The bus was hired by the ladies for its picnic.
Our country needs a number of self-sacrificing and devoted political leaders.
At last he was married to a poor girl.
explanationEnglish: The plural antecedent 'ladies' requires the plural possessive adjective 'their' instead of the singular 'its'.
subject: English

qEnglish: 52. Which is a correct sentence?
optionsEnglish: She and her mother is late.
She as well as her brother is late.
explanationEnglish: When subjects are linked via 'as well as', agreement is governed entirely by the first singular subject ('She'), hence 'is' is correct.
subject: English

qEnglish: 53. Which of the following sentences is correct?
optionsEnglish: They decided to talk it over at dinner.
He picked up the books and put it on the table.
He will not escape punishment unless he does not speak the truth.
Milk is the most perfect food in the world.
explanationEnglish: The first sentence is fully correct. The others have clear pronoun errors or incorrect double negatives.
subject: English
